Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting ‘$500,000’ for ‘$250,000’ if—Pub. L. 105–206, § 6005(e)(1)“(A) a husband and wife make a joint return for the taxable year of the sale or exchange of the property,“(B) either spouse meets the ownership requirements of subsection (a) with respect to such property,“(C) both spouses meet the use requirements of subsection (a) with respect to such property, and“(D) neither spouse is ineligible for the benefits of subsection (a) with respect to such property by reason of paragraph (3).”Subsec. (c)(1). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In the case of a sale or exchange to which this subsection applies, the ownership and use requirements of subsection (a) shall not apply and subsection (b)(3) shall not apply; but the amount of gain excluded from gross income under subsection (a) with respect to such sale or exchange shall not exceed—Pub. L. 105–206, § 6005(e)(2)“(A) the amount which bears the same ratio to the amount which would be so excluded under this section if such requirements had been met, as“(B) the shorter of—“(i) the aggregate periods, during the 5-year period ending on the date of such sale or exchange, such property has been owned and used by the taxpayer as the taxpayer’s principal residence, or“(ii) the period after the date of the most recent prior sale or exchange by the taxpayer to which subsection (a) applied and before the date of such sale or exchange,bears to 2 years.”1997— amended section catchline and text generally. Effective Date of 1997 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–34, title III, § 312(d)111 Stat. 841Pub. L. 105–206, title VI, § 6005(e)(3)112 Stat. 806Aug. 5, 1997July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 25, 32, 56, 143, 163, 215, 280A, 464, 512, 1016, 1033, 1038, 1223, 1250, 1274, 6012, 6045, 6212, 6334, 6504, and 7872 of this title and repealing ] shall apply to sales and exchanges after .section 1034 of this titleMay 6, 1997“(2).—Sales on or before date of enactmentAt the election of the taxpayer, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to any sale or exchange on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 5, 1997“(3).—Certain sales within 2 years after date of enactmentSection 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as amended by this section) shall be applied without regard to subsection (c)(2)(B) thereof in the case of any sale or exchange of property during the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act if the taxpayer held such property on the date of the enactment of this Act and fails to meet the ownership and use requirements of subsection (a) thereof with respect to such property.“(4).—Binding contractsAt the election of the taxpayer, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to a sale or exchange after the date of the enactment of this Act, if—“(A)such sale or exchange is pursuant to a contract which was binding on such date, or“(B)without regard to such amendments, gain would not be recognized under section 1034 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act) on such sale or exchange by reason of a new residence acquired on or before such date or with respect to the acquisition of which by the taxpayer a binding contract was in effect on such date.This paragraph shall not apply to any sale or exchange by an individual if the treatment provided by section 877(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 applies to such individual.”

Sense of Congress Concerning Tax Treatment of Principal Residence of Members of Armed Forces While Away From Home on Active Duty, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–261, div. A, title X, § 1074112 Stat. 2138Oct. 17, 1998“It is the sense of Congress that a member of the Armed Forces should be treated for purposes of section 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as using property as a principal residence during any continuous period that the member is serving on active duty for 180 days or more with the Armed Forces, but only if the member used the property as a principal residence for any period during or immediately before that period of active duty.”

Transitional Rule in Case of Sale or Exchange of Residence BeforeJuly 26, 1981, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–600, title IV, § 404(d)(2)92 Stat. 2870Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“In the case of a sale or exchange of a residence before , a taxpayer who has attained age 65 on the date of such sale or exchange may elect to have section 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] applied by substituting ‘8-year period’ for ‘5-year period’ and ‘5 years’ for ‘3 years’ in subsections (a), (d)(2), and (d)(5) of such section.”July 26, 1981

Effective Date of 1997 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–34, title III, § 312(d)111 Stat. 841Pub. L. 105–206, title VI, § 6005(e)(3)112 Stat. 806Aug. 5, 1997July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 25, 32, 56, 143, 163, 215, 280A, 464, 512, 1016, 1033, 1038, 1223, 1250, 1274, 6012, 6045, 6212, 6334, 6504, and 7872 of this title and repealing ] shall apply to sales and exchanges after .section 1034 of this titleMay 6, 1997“(2).—Sales on or before date of enactmentAt the election of the taxpayer, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to any sale or exchange on or before the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 5, 1997“(3).—Certain sales within 2 years after date of enactmentSection 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as amended by this section) shall be applied without regard to subsection (c)(2)(B) thereof in the case of any sale or exchange of property during the 2-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act if the taxpayer held such property on the date of the enactment of this Act and fails to meet the ownership and use requirements of subsection (a) thereof with respect to such property.“(4).—Binding contractsAt the election of the taxpayer, the amendments made by this section shall not apply to a sale or exchange after the date of the enactment of this Act, if—“(A)such sale or exchange is pursuant to a contract which was binding on such date, or“(B)without regard to such amendments, gain would not be recognized under section 1034 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act) on such sale or exchange by reason of a new residence acquired on or before such date or with respect to the acquisition of which by the taxpayer a binding contract was in effect on such date.This paragraph shall not apply to any sale or exchange by an individual if the treatment provided by section 877(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 applies to such individual.”

Sense of Congress Concerning Tax Treatment of Principal Residence of Members of Armed Forces While Away From Home on Active Duty, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–261, div. A, title X, § 1074112 Stat. 2138Oct. 17, 1998“It is the sense of Congress that a member of the Armed Forces should be treated for purposes of section 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as using property as a principal residence during any continuous period that the member is serving on active duty for 180 days or more with the Armed Forces, but only if the member used the property as a principal residence for any period during or immediately before that period of active duty.”

Transitional Rule in Case of Sale or Exchange of Residence BeforeJuly 26, 1981, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–600, title IV, § 404(d)(2)92 Stat. 2870Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“In the case of a sale or exchange of a residence before , a taxpayer who has attained age 65 on the date of such sale or exchange may elect to have section 121 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] applied by substituting ‘8-year period’ for ‘5-year period’ and ‘5 years’ for ‘3 years’ in subsections (a), (d)(2), and (d)(5) of such section.”July 26, 1981

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: a Exclusion
children.text: Gross income shall not include gain from the sale or exchange of property if, during the 5-year period ending on the date of the sale or exchange, such property has been owned and used by the taxpayer as the taxpayer’s principal residence for periods aggregating 2 years or more.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of gain excluded from gross income under subsection (a) with respect to any sale or exchange shall not exceed $250,000.

children.heading: $500,000 Limitation for certain joint returns
children.label: A $500,000 Limitation for certain joint returns
i
children.text: either spouse meets the ownership requirements of subsection (a) with respect to such property;

children.label: ii
children.text: both spouses meet the use requirements of subsection (a) with respect to such property; and

children.label: iii
children.text: neither spouse is ineligible for the benefits of subsection (a) with respect to such property by reason of paragraph (3).

children.heading: Other joint returns
children.label: B Other joint returns
children.text: If such spouses do not meet the requirements of subparagraph (A), the limitation under paragraph (1) shall be the sum of the limitations under paragraph (1) to which each spouse would be entitled if such spouses had not been married. For purposes of the preceding sentence, each spouse shall be treated as owning the property during the period that either spouse owned the property.

children.heading: Application to only 1 sale or exchange every 2 years
children.label: 3 Application to only 1 sale or exchange every 2 years
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to any sale or exchange by the taxpayer if, during the 2-year period ending on the date of such sale or exchange, there was any other sale or exchange by the taxpayer to which subsection (a) applied.

children.heading: Special rule for certain sales by surviving spouses
children.label: 4 Special rule for certain sales by surviving spouses
children.text: In the case of a sale or exchange of property by an unmarried individual whose spouse is deceased on the date of such sale, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “$500,000” for “$250,000” if such sale occurs not later than 2 years after the date of death of such spouse and the requirements of paragraph (2)(A) were met immediately before such date of death.

children.heading: Exclusion of gain allocated to nonqualified use
children.label: 5 Exclusion of gain allocated to nonqualified use

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to so much of the gain from the sale or exchange of property as is allocated to periods of nonqualified use.

children.heading: Gain allocated to periods of nonqualified use
children.label: B Gain allocated to periods of nonqualified use
i
children.text: the aggregate periods of nonqualified use during the period such property was owned by the taxpayer, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the period such property was owned by the taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “period of nonqualified use” means any period (other than the portion of any period preceding ) during which the property is not used as the principal residence of the taxpayer or the taxpayer’s spouse or former spouse.January 1, 2009

children.label: I
children.text: any portion of the 5-year period described in subsection (a) which is after the last date that such property is used as the principal residence of the taxpayer or the taxpayer’s spouse,

children.label: II
children.text: any period (not to exceed an aggregate period of 10 years) during which the taxpayer or the taxpayer’s spouse is serving on qualified official extended duty (as defined in subsection (d)(9)(C)) described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii) of subsection (d)(9)(A), and

children.label: III
children.text: any other period of temporary absence (not to exceed an aggregate period of 2 years) due to change of employment, health conditions, or such other unforeseen circumstances as may be specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Coordination with recognition of gain attributable to depreciation
children.label: D Coordination with recognition of gain attributable to depreciation
i
children.text: subparagraph (A) shall be applied after the application of subsection (d)(6), and

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (B) shall be applied without regard to any gain to which subsection (d)(6) applies.

children.heading: Exclusion for taxpayers failing to meet certain requirements
children.label: c Exclusion for taxpayers failing to meet certain requirements
A
children.text: the amount which bears the same ratio to such limitation (determined without regard to this paragraph) as

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate periods, during the 5-year period ending on the date of such sale or exchange, such property has been owned and used by the taxpayer as the taxpayer’s principal residence; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period after the date of the most recent prior sale or exchange by the taxpayer to which subsection (a) applied and before the date of such sale or exchange, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 years.

children.heading: Sales and exchanges to which subsection applies
children.label: 2 Sales and exchanges to which subsection applies
i
children.text: a failure to meet the ownership and use requirements of subsection (a), or

children.label: ii
children.text: subsection (b)(3), and

children.label: B
children.text: such sale or exchange is by reason of a change in place of employment, health, or, to the extent provided in regulations, unforeseen circumstances.

children.heading: Joint returns
children.label: 1 Joint returns
children.text: If a husband and wife make a joint return for the taxable year of the sale or exchange of the property, subsections (a) and (c) shall apply if either spouse meets the ownership and use requirements of subsection (a) with respect to such property.

children.heading: Property of deceased spouse
children.label: 2 Property of deceased spouse
children.text: For purposes of this section, in the case of an unmarried individual whose spouse is deceased on the date of the sale or exchange of property, the period such unmarried individual owned and used such property shall include the period such deceased spouse owned and used such property before death.

children.heading: Property owned by spouse or former spouse
children.label: 3 Property owned by spouse or former spouse

children.heading: Property transferred to individual from spouse or former spouse
children.label: A Property transferred to individual from spouse or former spouse
children.text: In the case of an individual holding property transferred to such individual in a transaction described in section 1041(a), the period such individual owns such property shall include the period the transferor owned the property.

children.heading: Property used by former spouse pursuant to divorce decree, etc.
children.label: B Property used by former spouse pursuant to divorce decree, etc.
children.text: Solely for purposes of this section, an individual shall be treated as using property as such individual’s principal residence during any period of ownership while such individual’s spouse or former spouse is granted use of the property under a divorce or separation instrument.

children.label: i
children.text: a decree of divorce or separate maintenance or a written instrument incident to such a decree,

children.label: ii
children.text: a written separation agreement, or

children.label: iii
children.text: a decree (not described in clause (i)) requiring a spouse to make payments for the support or maintenance of the other spouse.

children.label: A
children.text: the holding requirements of subsection (a) shall be applied to the holding of such stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: the use requirements of subsection (a) shall be applied to the house or apartment which the taxpayer was entitled to occupy as such stockholder.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the destruction, theft, seizure, requisition, or condemnation of property shall be treated as the sale of such property.

children.heading: Application of section 1033
children.label: B Application of section 1033
children.text: In applying section 1033 (relating to involuntary conversions), the amount realized from the sale or exchange of property shall be treated as being the amount determined without regard to this section, reduced by the amount of gain not included in gross income pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Property acquired after involuntary conversion
children.label: C Property acquired after involuntary conversion
children.text: If the basis of the property sold or exchanged is determined (in whole or in part) under section 1033(b) (relating to basis of property acquired through involuntary conversion), then the holding and use by the taxpayer of the converted property shall be treated as holding and use by the taxpayer of the property sold or exchanged.

children.heading: Recognition of gain attributable to depreciation
children.label: 6 Recognition of gain attributable to depreciation
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to so much of the gain from the sale of any property as does not exceed the portion of the depreciation adjustments (as defined in section 1250(b)(3)) attributable to periods after , in respect of such property.May 6, 1997

children.heading: Determination of use during periods of out-of-residence care
children.label: 7 Determination of use during periods of out-of-residence care
A
children.text: becomes physically or mentally incapable of self-care, and

children.label: B
children.text: owns property and uses such property as the taxpayer’s principal residence during the 5-year period described in subsection (a) for periods aggregating at least 1 year,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: At the election of the taxpayer, this section shall not fail to apply to the sale or exchange of an interest in a principal residence by reason of such interest being a remainder interest in such residence, but this section shall not apply to any other interest in such residence which is sold or exchanged separately.

children.heading: Exception for sales to related parties
children.label: B Exception for sales to related parties
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any sale to, or exchange with, any person who bears a relationship to the taxpayer which is described in section 267(b) or 707(b).

children.heading: Uniformed services, Foreign Service, and intelligence community
children.label: 9 Uniformed services, Foreign Service, and intelligence community
i
children.text: as a member of the uniformed services,

children.label: ii
children.text: as a member of the Foreign Service of the United States, or

children.label: iii
children.text: as an employee of the intelligence community.

children.heading: Maximum period of suspension
children.label: B Maximum period of suspension
children.text: The 5-year period described in subsection (a) shall not be extended more than 10 years by reason of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “qualified official extended duty” means any extended duty while serving at a duty station which is at least 50 miles from such property or while residing under Government orders in Government quarters.

children.heading: Uniformed services
children.label: ii Uniformed services
children.text: The term “uniformed services” has the meaning given such term by , United States Code, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this paragraph.section 101(a)(5) of title 10

children.heading: Foreign Service of the United States
children.label: iii Foreign Service of the United States
children.text: The term “member of the Foreign Service of the United States” has the meaning given the term “member of the Service” by paragraph (1), (2), (3), (4), or (5) of section 103 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: the Office of the Director of National Intelligence,

children.label: II
children.text: the Central Intelligence Agency,

children.label: III
children.text: the National Security Agency,

children.label: IV
children.text: the Defense Intelligence Agency,

children.label: V
children.text: the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency,

children.label: VI
children.text: the National Reconnaissance Office,

children.label: VII
children.text: any other office within the Department of Defense for the collection of specialized national intelligence through reconnaissance programs,

children.label: VIII
children.text: any of the intelligence elements of the Army, the Navy, the Air Force, the Marine Corps, the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Department of Treasury, the Department of Energy, and the Coast Guard,

children.label: IX
children.text: the Bureau of Intelligence and Research of the Department of State, or

children.label: X
children.text: any of the elements of the Department of Homeland Security concerned with the analyses of foreign intelligence information.

children.heading: Extended duty
children.label: v Extended duty
children.text: The term “extended duty” means any period of active duty pursuant to a call or order to such duty for a period in excess of 90 days or for an indefinite period.

children.heading: Special rules relating to election
children.label: D Special rules relating to election

children.heading: Election limited to 1 property at a time
children.label: i Election limited to 1 property at a time
children.text: An election under subparagraph (A) with respect to any property may not be made if such an election is in effect with respect to any other property.

children.heading: Revocation of election
children.label: ii Revocation of election
children.text: An election under subparagraph (A) may be revoked at any time.

children.heading: Property acquired in like-kind exchange
children.label: 10 Property acquired in like-kind exchange
children.text: If a taxpayer acquires property in an exchange with respect to which gain is not recognized (in whole or in part) to the taxpayer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 1031, subsection (a) shall not apply to the sale or exchange of such property by such taxpayer (or by any person whose basis in such property is determined, in whole or in part, by reference to the basis in the hands of such taxpayer) during the 5-year period beginning with the date of such acquisition.

children.label: i
children.text: on qualified official extended duty (as defined in paragraph (9)(C)) as an employee of the Peace Corps, or

children.label: ii
children.text: as an enrolled volunteer or volunteer leader under section 5 or 6 (as the case may be) of the Peace Corps Act (, 2505).22 U.S.C. 2504

children.heading: Applicable rules
children.label: B Applicable rules
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (B) and (D) of paragraph (9) shall apply.

children.heading: Denial of exclusion for expatriates
children.label: e Denial of exclusion for expatriates
children.text: This section shall not apply to any sale or exchange by an individual if the treatment provided by section 877(a)(1) applies to such individual.

children.heading: Election to have section not apply
children.label: f Election to have section not apply
children.text: This section shall not apply to any sale or exchange with respect to which the taxpayer elects not to have this section apply.

children.heading: Residences acquired in rollovers under section 1034
children.label: g Residences acquired in rollovers under section 1034
children.text: For purposes of this section, in the case of property the acquisition of which by the taxpayer resulted under section 1034 (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this section) in the nonrecognition of any part of the gain realized on the sale or exchange of another residence, in determining the period for which the taxpayer has owned and used such property as the taxpayer’s principal residence, there shall be included the aggregate periods for which such other residence (and each prior residence taken into account under section 1223(6) in determining the holding period of such property) had been so owned and used.11See References in Text note below.1
